title: 10 Top-Rated Herbal Creams for Urinary Calculus Prevention

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols, also known as dandelion, are good for urinary calculus because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of kidney stones by flushing out minerals and toxins from the body.

Tannins, also known as Dandelion, are good for urinary calculus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the pain and inflammation associated with kidney stones, while also helping to prevent the formation of new stones by reducing the concentration of minerals that can combine to form stones.
Terpinen-4-ol is good for urinary calculus because it has antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause kidney stone formation and infections.

Flavonoids (Dandelion) are good for urinary calculus because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the size and number of kidney stones by preventing the formation of new stones and making existing ones easier to pass.

Quercetin (common dandelion) is good for urinary calculus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with kidney stones, also preventing them from forming due to its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that contribute to stone formation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Juniperus communis cream is often used to help with urinary calculus, also known as kidney stones. This is because the cream contains compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease pain. The Juniperus communis plant has been used in traditional medicine for a long time to treat various health problems, including issues with the urinary system. The cream is said to have diuretic properties, which means it helps to increase urine production and make it easier to pass stones. This is helpful because it allows the body to get rid of the stone more quickly. The cream may also have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort caused by the stone. By reducing swelling and pain, and helping the body to get rid of the stone, Juniperus communis cream may provide relief for people suffering from urinary calculus.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal cream for urinary calculus, start by gathering fresh dandelion flowers and leaves. Chop them finely and mix them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, in a 1:2 ratio. Let the mixture infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container regularly. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the dandelion oil with a base cream, such as beeswax and shea butter, in a 1:1 ratio. Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax melts, then let it cool and thicken. Once the cream has cooled, add a few drops of a natural preservative, such as vitamin E oil, to prevent spoilage. Store the Taraxacum officinale cream in a cool, dark place and apply it topically to the affected area 2-3 times a day, following a doctor's advice for best results.
remedies.image_alt: Juniperus communis herbal creams for urinary calculus

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Berberine, a natural compound found in dandelion, is good for urinary calculus because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce the formation of kidney stones by preventing the growth of bacteria that can cause infections and worsen stone formation.

Tannins, also known as Dandelion, are good for urinary calculus because they help to prevent the formation of kidney stones by reducing the concentration of minerals in urine and preventing them from sticking together.

Polyphenols, also known as dandelion, are good for urinary calculus because they help to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of kidney stones by dissolving existing ones and improving urine flow, which in turn helps to flush out toxins and minerals that can cause stone formation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Berberis vulgaris cream is a natural remedy that has been used to help dissolve kidney stones, also known as urinary calculus. The cream is made from the root of the Berberis plant, which contains a special type of alkaloid that helps to break down the stones. When applied topically to the skin over the kidney area, the cream is absorbed into the bloodstream and carried to the kidneys, where it helps to dissolve the stones. This is because Berberis vulgaris has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the urinary tract. It also helps to flush out the stones from the body, reducing the risk of complications such as infection or blockage. By using Berberis vulgaris cream, people may be able to avoid more invasive medical treatments and reduce their discomfort and pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Taraxacum officinale herbal cream that helps with urinary calculus, first, you'll need to prepare the herbal infusion. This involves gently pouring 250ml of boiling water over 2 tablespoons of dried Taraxacum officinale root and leaves, then allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes. Next, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. You'll then need to combine the herbal infusion with 100g of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture gently until it reaches a temperature of around 40-50 degrees Celsius, then remove it from the heat and let it cool. Once cooled, mix in 10g of a natural emulsifier, such as beeswax or candelilla wax, until it's fully incorporated. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean glass jar, allow it to cool and solidify, and your Taraxacum officinale herbal cream is ready to use. It can be applied topically to the lower abdominal area to help alleviate symptoms of urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Berberis vulgaris herbal creams for urinary calculus

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense cream is a natural remedy that has been used to help with urinary calculus, which is a type of kidney stone. The cream is made from the plant Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health problems. The active ingredients in Equisetum arvense cream, such as flavonoids and saponins, are thought to help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the urinary tract. This can help to prevent the formation of kidney stones and reduce the symptoms associated with them, such as pain and discomfort. The cream is also believed to help to break down existing stones, making it easier to pass them out of the body through urination. Additionally, the cream has diuretic properties, which can help to increase urine production and flush out the kidneys, further reducing the risk of kidney stones. By using Equisetum arvense cream, people may be able to prevent or alleviate the symptoms of urinary calculus, and reduce their risk of developing more serious health problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal creams that help with urinary calculus, you'll need fresh dandelion flowers and leaves, coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax. First, dry the dandelion flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven or with a dehydrator to preserve their medicinal properties. Next, make a herbal infusion by steeping 1 tablespoon of dried dandelion flowers and leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let the mixture cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Combine the herbal infusion with 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of melted beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated, then remove it from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency similar to thin honey. Pour the mixture into small containers or jars and let it cool completely before using the Taraxacum officinale herbal cream as needed to help alleviate symptoms of urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal creams for urinary calculus

remedies.remedy_desc: Arctium lappa, also known as Burdock root, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to help with various health issues. The cream made from this plant is believed to be beneficial for urinary calculus, which is a condition where small stones form in the kidneys or bladder. The cream may help to break down the stones by dissolving the minerals that make them up. This is due to the presence of a compound called inulin, which is a type of soluble fiber that can help to flush out the stones and their fragments from the body. Additionally, the cream may also help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with the condition. This is because Burdock root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the affected area. The cream may also have a mild diuretic effect, which can help to increase urine production and flush out the stones more quickly.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal creams that help with urinary calculus, start by gathering fresh dandelion roots and leaves, as they contain taraxasterol, a compound that may help dissolve kidney stones. Clean and dry the herbs, then chop them into small pieces. Combine the chopped herbs with a carrier oil like olive or coconut oil in a saucepan, and heat them gently over low heat for 2-3 hours to create a herbal infusion. After the infusion is cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, and discard the solids. Next, mix the herbal infusion with a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax, and add a small amount of essential oil like peppermint or lavender to give the cream a pleasant scent. Finally, blend the mixture well and pour it into a container, allowing it to cool and solidify before use. The resulting Taraxacum officinale herbal cream can be applied topically to the lower back or abdomen, where it may help to reduce inflammation and promote the passage of kidney stones, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for this purpose.

remedies.remedy_desc: Juniperus oxycedrus cream is a traditional remedy that has been used for centuries to help with urinary problems, including stones or calculi. The cream is made from the extract of the Juniperus oxycedrus plant, which is a type of juniper tree. The active ingredients in the cream, such as terpenoids and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract, while also increasing urine production to help flush out stones or other debris. By promoting urination and reducing inflammation, Juniperus oxycedrus cream can help to break down and remove small stones or prevent new stones from forming. This can help to relieve symptoms such as pain, discomfort, and difficulty urinating. Additionally, the cream's diuretic properties can help to prevent the formation of new stones by keeping the urine diluted and reducing the concentration of minerals that can contribute to stone formation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Taraxacum officinale herbal cream that helps with urinary calculus, start by gathering fresh dandelion flowers, leaves, and roots. You can grow these yourself in a well-drained area or purchase them from a local herbal store. Dry the plant parts by tying them in small bundles and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, dark place. Once the plant material is completely dry, grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the powder with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1 part plant material to 10 parts oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the plant properties. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to the liquid, stirring until it has melted and the mixture has thickened to your desired consistency. Let the cream cool and solidify before applying it to the affected area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The creams made from its leaves are believed to be beneficial for urinary calculus, which are hard stones that form in the urinary tract. The leaves of the plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help reduce the inflammation and pain associated with urinary calculus. The creams may also help reduce the size of the stones, making it easier to pass them out of the body. Additionally, the compounds in Urtica dioica may help prevent the formation of new stones by reducing the levels of minerals that contribute to their development. Some people believe that the creams also help dissolve the stones, although more research is needed to confirm this.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal cream that helps with urinary calculus, you will need dried dandelion root and leaf, olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, combine 1/4 cup of dried dandelion root and leaf in a clean glass jar, then pour 1 cup of olive oil over them. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax in the double boiler, then slowly add the infused oil to the melted wax, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens and cools. Pour the Taraxacum officinale herbal cream into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator, as it can spoil easily. This cream can be applied topically to help alleviate discomfort and support the removal of urinary stones. However, please consult a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon creams contain compounds found in cranberries, which have been shown to be helpful in preventing and treating urinary calculus, also known as kidney stones. The main compound, proanthocyanidin, helps to stop bacteria from sticking to the walls of the bladder and kidneys, where they can cause infections and stone formation. By preventing these infections, Vaccinium macrocarpon creams can reduce the risk of developing new stones or the recurrence of old ones. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Vaccinium macrocarpon creams may also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones. Some studies suggest that Vaccinium macrocarpon creams may be particularly beneficial in preventing the formation of stones caused by oxalate, which is a common type of kidney stone. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal cream for urinary calculus, you will need dandelion root, beeswax, coconut oil, and a double boiler. Start by drying and grinding the dandelion root into a fine powder. In a double boiler, melt the coconut oil and add the dandelion root powder, stirring well to combine. Continue to heat the mixture until the dandelion root is fully incorporated and the mixture is hot but not boiling. Next, add the beeswax to the mixture and stir until it is fully melted and combined. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before transferring it to a glass jar. As it cools, the mixture will thicken and become a cream. This herbal cream can be applied topically to the affected area to help reduce inflammation and promote the passage of urinary stones. It is essential to note that while Taraxacum officinale has been traditionally used to help with urinary calculus, it should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. intro_desc: Herbal creams can be helpful in treating urinary calculus, also known as kidney stones. This condition occurs when minerals in the urine form hard stones that can cause severe pain and discomfort. Two herbs that have been traditionally used to treat urinary calculus are Juniperus communis and Berberis vulgaris. Juniperus communis, also known as juniper, is a natural diuretic that helps to increase urine production and flush out the stones. Berberis vulgaris, also known as barberry, contains a compound called berberine, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce the risk of infection and promote healing. Using herbal creams that contain these herbs can help to alleviate symptoms and prevent the formation of new stones.  Urinary calculus can be a serious health issue that affects not only the kidneys, but also the urinary tract and other parts of the body. If left untreated, the stones can cause damage to the kidneys and increase the risk of complications such as urinary tract infections, kidney damage, and even kidney failure. Therefore, it's essential to take steps to prevent the formation of stones and treat the condition promptly if it occurs. By using herbal creams that contain Juniperus communis and Berberis vulgaris, individuals can take a natural and effective approach to treating urinary calculus and improving their overall health.  Improving your life by treating urinary calculus is crucial because the condition can cause significant pain, discomfort, and emotional distress. It can also lead to a decrease in quality of life, making everyday activities a challenge. By addressing the issue, individuals can restore their normal urine flow, alleviate pain, and improve their overall well-being. They can also reduce their risk of developing complications and enjoy a better quality of life. In addition, treating urinary calculus can also help to prevent other health issues that may be related to the condition, such as urinary tract infections and kidney damage. By taking proactive steps to address the condition, individuals can improve their health and well-being, and enjoy a more active and fulfilling life.
supplementary_best: To remedy urinary calculus, a combination of Juniperus communis, Berberis vulgaris, and Urtica dioica is effective. Juniperus communis is known to improve urine flow and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. Berberis vulgaris helps to break down and eliminate kidney stones by increasing urine acidity and reducing mineral deposits. Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle root, has been shown to inhibit the growth of kidney stones by blocking the formation of calcium oxalate crystals. This combination works well because Juniperus communis addresses the symptoms of urinary calculus, while Berberis vulgaris and Urtica dioica target the root cause of the condition by preventing the formation and growth of stones.
supplementary_avoid: If you have urinary calculus, it's best to avoid herbal creams containing Saw Palmetto (Serenoa repens), Licorice Root (Glycyrrhiza glabra), and Dandelion Root (Taraxacum's close relative) are not mentioned. However,  some herbal creams contain other herbs that may worsen your condition. For example, herbal creams with Angelica (Angelica sinensis) should be avoided because they can increase urine production, which might dislodge kidney stones into the urinary tract. Also, herbal creams containing Horsetail (Equisetum's close relative) should be avoided because they can increase the risk of kidney stone formation by increasing urine acidity. Similarly, herbal creams containing Ginkgo Biloba should be avoided because they can increase urine production and decrease blood clotting, which might be a problem if you have a kidney stone.
supplementary_other_remedies: Drinking plenty of water is a natural remedy that can help with urinary calculus. When you urinate, the extra water helps to flush out the minerals that cause stones to form. Cranberry juice and other fluids that are rich in citrate can also be helpful, as citrate can prevent the formation of stones by binding to minerals like calcium. Some people find that eating more foods that are high in citrate, such as citrus fruits and leafy greens, can also help to prevent stones from forming. Apple cider vinegar has also been used to help break down and dissolve stones, but it is essential to drink it in moderation and talk to a doctor before making any changes to your diet.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have urinary calculus (kidney stones) may also experience other health issues. One common problem is urinary tract infections (UTIs). The stones can cause blockages in the urinary tract, making it easier for bacteria to grow and cause infections. Medicinal herbs like uva ursi and juniper berries can help prevent UTIs by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria. Uva ursi has antiseptic properties that can help stop the growth of bacteria, while juniper berries can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.  Another issue people with urinary calculus may face is chronic inflammation and pain in the lower back and abdomen. The stones can cause inflammation in the kidneys and urinary tract, leading to pain and discomfort. Medicinal herbs like marshmallow root and dandelion root can help reduce inflammation and ease pain. Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated tissues, while dandelion root can help reduce fluid retention and ease pressure on the kidneys.  People with urinary calculus may also experience digestive issues like constipation or diarrhea. The stones can cause blockages in the urinary tract, leading to changes in bowel habits. Medicinal herbs like licorice root and ginger can help regulate digestion and ease symptoms. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract, while ginger can help reduce nausea and ease digestion.  Lastly, people with urinary calculus may experience fatigue and weakness. The pain and discomfort caused by the stones can lead to fatigue and a general feeling of being unwell. Medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng can help boost energy and reduce fatigue. Ashwagandha has adaptogenic properties that can help the body adapt to stress, while ginseng can help increase energy and reduce fatigue.  These medicinal herbs can be used to help prevent or alleviate these health issues, but it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new supplements or treatments.
